rulings.Case Title: PROFESSIONAL COLLECTION CONSULTANTS (PCC) VS. ROBERT M. LUJAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Attorney Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 17, 2018, Line 1, DEFENDANT ROBERT LUJAN's Motion For Attorney Fees And Costs Defendant Lujan's motion for attorney fees and costs on appeal is GRANTED in the amount of $105,459. The appeal yielded a published decision in an important field of law. I reviewed counsels' time entries and hourly rates and find them reasonable based on my 32 years' experience as a litigator and then judge in this legal market. (See Serrano v. Priest (1977) 20 Cal.3d 25, 49.) A multiplier of 1.5 to the lodestar amount was applied in the trial court proceedings and affirmed by the Court of Appeal; I find the same multiplier appropriate on appeal. The award is calculated thus: $65,360 attorney fees x 1.5 ($98,040) + $7,200 in fees for this motion + $219 in costs = $105,459. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Lujan is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD WAYNE KOZEL VS. AIRLINE COACH SERVICE, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO ADMISSION/INTERROGATORIES
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 17, 2018, Line 1, DEFENDANT AIRLINE COACH SERVICE, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO ADMISSION/INTERROGATORIES Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted. Defendant shall serve code compliant responses to Form Interrogatory 17.1 without objection within 10 court days of entry of this order. Sanctions are awarded against Defendant in the amount of $1,890. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tomcohen@ionix.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MAREN MOUHANNA ET AL VS. LUCILE PACKARD CHILDREN'S HOSPITAL AT STANFORD ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 17, 2018, Line 4, DEFENDANTs SUTTER PACIFIC MEDICAL FOUNDATION and SUTTER BAY HOSPITALS DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT The Sutter defendants' demurrers to the first amended complaint's (FAC) causes of action are all OVERRULED. The FAC adequately alleges that Mr. Mouhanna was in a physician-patient relationship in that his blood was taken, genetic testing was performed on it, defendants provided him genetic counseling and thus he was a direct victim. Nothing like this was alleged in the opinion defendants rely on: Burgess v. Sup. Ct. (1992) 2 Cal.4th 1064 (or in any other cited case). Moreover, different theories of negligence liability are often pled in separate counts. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: ANGEL ALCARAZ VS. GUO FU CHEN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Angel Alcaraz'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 17, 2018, Line 6, PLAINTIFF ANGEL ALCARAZ' Motion For Protective Order Plaintiff's motion for a protective order is GRANTED. Defendants seek discovery of plaintiff's private medical and financial information, but refuse to enter a protective order. Defense counsel argues that his promise not to misuse the private information suffices, but such a promise could be made in virtually any case. Plaintiff's counsel is to submit a proposed protective order after review by defense counsel. rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER SUE THOMPSON VS. MARY JANE FRANUS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Special Motion And Special Motion To Dismiss (Code Civ. Proc. Sec. 425.16); Memorandum In Support; Declaration Of Mary Jan Franus; Declaration Of Mary Beth Norum;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 17, 2018, Line 7, DEFENDANT MARY FRANUS' Special Motion To Dismiss (Code Civ. Proc. Sec. 425.16) Defendant's CCP §425.16 anti-SLAPP motion is GRANTED. Defendant satisfies the first prong of §425.16; her filing of a complaint with the San Francisco Department of Police Accountability (SFDPA) is protected by the statute. (See, e.g., Walker v. Kiousis (2001) 93 Cal.App.4th 1432, 1439; Lee v. Fick (2005) 135 Cal.App.4th 89, 97.) Plaintiff argues that the first prong is not met because defendant's activity was a crime - filing "an allegation of misconduct against a police officer knowing the allegation to be false" (Penal Code §148.6). However, plaintiff fails to bear the high burden "of conclusively proving the illegal conduct." (See Cross v. Cooper (2011) 197 Cal.App.4th 357, 385; emphasis added.) In particular, plaintiff does not conclusively prove the knowledge element of §148.6 - defendant's complaint must have been knowingly false. (See id.) Defendant's declaration demonstrates that, while her complaint to the SFDPA may have been in some respects mistaken, she did not know that to be so. (See id.) Plaintiff's argument to the contrary is largely ipse dixit. On the second prong of §425.16, plaintiff must establish a "probability" that she will prevail on her pleaded claims. (Id.) However, Civil Code §47(b) provides a privilege for any statement in official proceedings such as those conducted by the SFDPA. While acknowledging this "absolute privilege," plaintiff cites Civil Code §47.5, which allows a police officer to sue for defamation over a false complaint. Again, though, the complaint must have been made "with knowledge that it was false." (Id.) Hence, plaintiff fails to establish the second prong of CCP §425.16. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Alcaraz is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu
